title: Interface controlled spin filtering and nonreciprocal transport in Altermagnet/Ising superconductor junctions
authors: Arindam Boruah, Saumen Acharjee, Prasanta Kumar Saikia
abstract: We investigate theoretically spin-resolved transport, spin filtering, and nonreciprocal effects in an Altermagnet/Ising superconductor (AM/ISC) junction with a spin-active interface. Using a modified Bogoliubov-de Gennes framework within the scattering formalism, we demonstrate that the interplay among intrinsic spin-orbit coupling (ISOC), anisotropic AM spin texture and spin-dependent interfacial scattering gives rise to strongly anisotropic charge and spin conductance. In the weak spin-mixing regime, transport remains predominantly helicity conserving and exhibits pronounced angular dependence governed by the relative orientation between the AM spin texture and interface magnetization. Increasing ISOC enhances spin conductance and leads to spin-selective Andreev reflection resulting in finite spin filtering. In contrast, the strong spin-mixing regime exhibits enhanced angular anisotropy and robust spin-polarized transport over a broad energy range. Conventional Andreev reflection becomes strongly suppressed, accompanied by substantial spectral redistribution. We further show that nonreciprocal transport persists throughout the single-band, intermediate and double-band ISC regime. The spin polarization and spin-filter efficiency exhibit nonmonotonic dependence on system parameters, reaching values up to $\sim 86\%$, with characteristic angular modulation determined by the AM spin texture. Finite-energy analysis reveals enhanced spin selectivity at low energies and suppression near the superconducting gap. Furthermore, strong spin mixing at the AM/ISC junction produces asymmetric conductance patterns, indicating nonreciprocal transport. Our results establish AM/ISC junctions as a versatile platform for tunable superconducting spintronics and directional spin transport.
summary: This paper explores how the unique spin textures of altermagnets and Ising superconductors can be harnessed at a spin-active interface to create highly efficient spin filters. It shows that by tuning the interface magnetization and the altermagnet orientation, one can achieve significant spin polarization and direction-dependent (nonreciprocal) transport, providing a platform for superconducting spintronics.
main_problem: Investigating how the interplay between momentum-dependent spin splitting in altermagnets and intrinsic spin-orbit coupling in Ising superconductors can be used to control spin-polarized transport and nonreciprocal effects via a spin-active interface.
main_result: The study demonstrates that a spin-active interface enables tunable spin filtering (up to 86% efficiency) and nonreciprocal transport, with transport properties highly dependent on the relative orientation of the altermagnet spin texture and interface magnetization.
method: A modified Bogoliubov-de Gennes (BdG) framework within the Blonder-Tinkham-Klapwijk (BTK) scattering formalism is used to calculate spin-resolved conductance and symmetry analysis.
model_or_system: A heterostructure junction consisting of an Altermagnet (AM) with d-wave symmetry exchange field and an Ising Superconductor (ISC) with strong spin-orbit coupling, separated by a spin-active interface with spin-dependent potential.
key_observables: Spin-resolved charge and spin conductance, spin polarization (P), spin-filter efficiency (eta), and nonreciprocal transport signatures.
important_parameters: AM orientation angle (delta), interface magnetization angle (chi_m), spin-mixing strength (rho), and Ising spin-orbit coupling strength (beta).
main_assumptions: The altermagnet exchange field is assumed to lie in the xy-plane; the interface is modeled as a delta-function potential.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the heterostructure schematic; Fig 2 shows conductance spectra vs AM orientation; Fig 5 shows conductance density plots across different ISOC regimes; Fig 6 shows angular modulation of spin polarization and efficiency; Fig 7 shows variation of parameters.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the AM/ISC junction, defines the Hamiltonian and interface model, derives the transport formalism, analyzes transport in different ISOC regimes, and discusses spin-filtering efficiency and nonreciprocity.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: (a) Schematic illustration of a heterostructure consisting of an Altermagnet (AM) and an Ising Superconductor (ISC), separated by a spin active interface at x = 0. The interface is characterized by a magnetic barrier strength (ρ) and orientation specified by the polar angle (χm) and azimuthal angle (ξm) respectively. The pa- rameter φ denotes the crystallographic orientation of the AM, which effectively rotates the spin-split Fermi surfaces corresponding to ma- jority (blue) and minority (red) spin channels. (b) Schematic energy band structure of AM illustrating momentum-dependent spin split- ting. The black dashed line corresponds to Fermi energy of the AM. (c) Schematic energy...

FIG. 2: Charge conductance spectra for different AM orientation angles (δ) considering Z0 = 0.1 and ξm = 45◦. Panels (a)–(c), (d)–(f), and (g)–(i) correspond to single band ISC (β = 0.5µS), intermediate (β = µS) and double band ISC (β = 2µS) respectively. The interface magnetic moment is ρ = 0 and orientation angle χm = 0◦in the left column representing non-magnetic barrier while for the middle and right column are for magnetic barrier with ρ = 0.1 considering χm = 15◦and 45◦respectively.

Fig. 2 presents the variation of charge conductance with quasiparticle energy E/∆0 for different AM orientations δ and spin-active parameters ρ and χm considering Z0 = 0.1 and ξm = 45◦. It is to be noted that we fix ξm and Z0 through- out this work, since variations in ξm produce only minor quan- titative changes without altering the qualitative transport be- havior. A small but finite scalar barrier is chosen to main- tain sufficient interface transparency, as larger Z0 suppresses Andreev reflection and weakens the spin-active interfacial ef- fects. In absence of spin-active interfaces i.e., for ρ = 0 and χm = 0, the conductance spectra remain nearly indepen- dent of δ for all combinations...

FIG. 3: Spin conductance spectra for different AM orientation angles (δ) considering Z0 = 0.1 and ξm = 45◦. Panels (a)–(c), (d)–(f), and (g)–(i) correspond to single band ISC (β = 0.5µS), intermediate (β = µS), and double band ISC (β = 2µS) respectively. The interface magnetic moment is ρ = 0 and orientation angle χm = 0◦in the left column representing non-magnetic barrier while for the middle and right column are for magnetic barrier with ρ = 0.1 considering χm = 15◦and 45◦respectively.

FIG. 4: (Top panel) Charge conductance spectra, (Bottom panel) Spin conductance spectra for different values of δ considering ρ = 0.5 and χm = 30◦with Z0 = 0.1 and ξm = 45◦. Panels (a)–(c) are for single band ISC (β = 0.5µS) while (d)–(f) are for double band ISC (β = 2µS) respectively.
